title: T-075 — supplier_specs Phase 1 governance and review states
prompt:
# T-075 — supplier_specs Phase 1 governance

Extend supplier_specs Phase 1 schema/actions so it can support internal upload/view/review/approval and RM usability without becoming a supplier portal. Required fields if absent: supplier_status, spec_version not null, effective_from, expiry_date, lifecycle_status `draft|active|expired|superseded|blocked`, review_status `pending|approved|rejected|blocked`, review_notes, cost_review_blocked, spec_review_blocked, approved_by, approved_at, rejected_by, rejected_at, rejection_reason, spec_document_url, document_sha256, document_mime_type, declared_allergens, declared_attrs, certificate_refs. Enforce at most one active approved non-expired spec per org,item,supplier through a partial unique index or equivalent safe constraint. Add Technical actions for upload metadata, view detail, submit review, approve, reject/block. RED first: tests prove expired/blocked/rejected specs cannot satisfy RM usability.

pipeline_inputs.details: Phase 1 is Technical-maintained master data with upload/view/review actions. Supplier portal upload, supplier negotiation and external document workflow are out of scope. PO actuals may reference observed spec data and create review/non-conformance but cannot mutate supplier_specs without explicit Technical approval path. Keep object storage integration minimal: persist metadata/url/hash, do not implement supplier portal.
acceptance_criteria: Given two active approved supplier_specs for same org,item,supplier overlap, then the database or service rejects the second active record.
Given expiry_date is in the past, then lifecycle_status resolves expired and RM usability cannot pass.
Given PO review proposes supplier spec change, then supplier_specs remains unchanged until approveSupplierSpecReview is called by Technical role.
Given supplier_status is blocked, then RM usability returns SUPPLIER_NOT_APPROVED.
Given Technical uploads supplier spec metadata/document reference, then record starts draft or pending review and is visible in supplier_specs list/detail.
Given reviewer approves supplier_spec, then lifecycle/review status allows RM usability only if non-expired and supplier_status is approved.
Given reviewer rejects/blocks supplier_spec, then RM usability fails with explicit reason and prior active approved spec is not overwritten.
test_strategy: Add RED migration tests for status enums and active uniqueness.
risk_red_lines: Do not automatically overwrite supplier_specs from PO actuals.
Do not remove declared_allergens compatibility with existing allergen cascade.
pipeline_inputs.description: supplier_specs Phase 1 governance fields, review states and uniqueness constraints.
